channel.title: Rl on RadixArk Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Rl on RadixArk Blog

item.title: Solving the Bellman Equation
item.description:
In Reinforcement Learning, an agent navigates a Markov Decision Process (MDP) to maximize a delayed reward. The math behind mapping state to action is governed by Richard Bellman’s principle of optimality.

The Value Function

The state-value function \( V^\pi(s) \) specifies the expected return starting from state \( s \), following policy \( \pi \):

$$ V^\pi(s) = \mathbb{E}_\pi \left[ \sum_{k=0}^{\infty} \gamma^k R_{t+k+1} \mid S_t = s \right] $$

Where \( \gamma \) is the discount factor (\( 0 \le \gamma \le 1 \)) and \( R \) is the reward.
